sections.heading: IF-THEN Rules
sections.body: A rule-based system encodes knowledge as IF-THEN rules: "IF condition THEN conclusion/action." For example, "IF temperature > 38°C THEN patient has fever" — the system fires this rule whenever its condition is satisfied by known facts.

sections.heading: Production Systems
sections.body: A production system is the general architecture underlying rule-based systems: a set of IF-THEN rules (production rules), a working memory holding current facts, and a control mechanism that decides which applicable rule to fire next.

sections.heading: Rule Engine
sections.body: The rule engine (or inference engine) is the component that matches rules against the current facts in working memory, determines which rules are applicable, and executes (fires) them — updating working memory with new facts or actions, which may in turn trigger further rules.

sections.heading: Conflict Resolution
sections.body: When multiple rules are applicable at the same time, conflict resolution decides which one fires first — common strategies include rule priority/salience, specificity (prefer more specific rules), or recency (prefer rules matching the most recently added facts).

quiz.question: A production rule is structured as:
What does the rule engine (inference engine) do?
options: Matches rules against current facts and fires applicable ones

quiz.question: Conflict resolution is needed when:
options: There are no rules at all
Multiple rules are applicable at the same time and one must be chosen to fire first
The system has no working memory
